game_name: Wizards & Warriors III: Kuros: Visions of Power

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Required to navigate town and collect money
Found throughout; used to purchase keys, food, health restoration, and information at inns
Return to corresponding guild for first class test (knight, thief, wizard)
Three character classes with three progression levels each; unlock new abilities and access
Improved weaponry; higher levels grant better combat options
Greater magic; higher levels unlock more magical access
Access to buildings through doors and windows; enables exploration of previously locked areas
Return to level 2 of respective guild for harder tests
Return to level 3 of respective guild for most difficult tests
Three princesses hidden in Piedup; each grants a gem for throne room access
Four gems total required; unlock hidden passage to castle throne room
Horizontal or vertical-scrolling boss fights required to progress class mastery
Final confrontation; requires four gems to access throne room

typical_relief_triggers: Completing guild boss test and obtaining new disguise
Finding gems and progressing toward throne room access
typical_tension_triggers: Guild boss encounters in scrolling challenge levels
Locked areas requiring appropriate class/statue combination
Health depletion without access to healing items
Death penalty (three lives only, no continues/passwords)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-driven with gated progression; tension spikes at guild boss encounters; relief comes from ability unlocks and map expansion; overall arc escalates through statue collection (bronze→silver→gold) and gem acquisition

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Multiple princesses hidden throughout Piedup; optional secondary objectives alongside main gem progression
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden passage to throne room unlocked by four secret gems; requires specific class abilities and princess/dragon encounters to fully access

difficulty_curve.early_game: Kuros begins weakened without weapons/armor; basic exploration and bronze statue collection; introduction to money and inn systems
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Silver statues and medium-difficulty guild bosses; expansion into all three realms (town, underworld, palace); princess rescue quests begin; character class mastery increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Gold statues with hardest guild tests; dragon boss encounter; final gem acquisition; threshold for throne room access; Malkil confrontation
notable_spikes: Bronze→Silver statue progression (increased boss difficulty per guild level)
Gold statue guild tests (described as 'most difficult')
Final gem acquisition and throne room passage

notable_level_patterns.description: Each statue return triggers a scrolling challenge level (horizontal or vertical) culminating in a boss fight; difficulty escalates with statue tier (bronze→silver→gold) within the same guild
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Knight guild level 1, 2, 3 progression; Wizard guild progression; Thief guild progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Town, underworld, and palace as three interconnected realms; freely navigable; each realm contains statues, princesses, money, and shops; ability gating restricts access until correct class disguise obtained
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Piedup town (hub), underworld (dragon location), palace/castle (throne room access)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each character class unlocks previously inaccessible areas; knight weaponry enables combat paths, wizard magic enables magical locks/obstacles, thief door/window access enables stealth routes
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Doors/windows only passable as thief; magical barriers only passable as wizard; physical obstacles requiring knight strength
